title: Vector Fundamentals
summary: Understand vectors as directed quantities, learn vector addition, subtraction, and scalar multiplication.
content_html:
A vector is a quantity with both magnitude and direction, in contrast to a scalar (magnitude only).

Notation and Components

A 2D vector v = (v₁, v₂) or column form:

v = [3]
    [4]

The magnitude (length): |v| = √(v₁² + v₂²) = √(9+16) = 5

Vector Operations

Addition:      u + v = (u₁+v₁, u₂+v₂)
Subtraction:   u - v = (u₁-v₁, u₂-v₂)
Scalar mult:   k·v = (k·v₁, k·v₂)

Example: u=(1,2), v=(3,4) → u+v = (4,6)

Unit Vector

A unit vector has magnitude 1. To normalize: v̂ = v/|v|

v = (3,4), |v| = 5
v̂ = (3/5, 4/5) = (0.6, 0.8)

Special Unit Vectors

  • i = (1,0) — x-axis unit vector
  • j = (0,1) — y-axis unit vector
  • k = (0,0,1) — z-axis unit vector

Any 3D vector: v = v₁i + v₂j + v₃k
